scope_note: QA Langlands Cap — CM Frobenius Ramanujan Equality cert [403]. Primary sources: Hecke, E. (1920) 'Eine neue Art von Zetafunktionen' Mathematische Zeitschrift 6:11-51 DOI: doi:10.1007/BF01458074 (Hecke L-functions and Grossencharacters); Shimura, G. (1971) 'Introduction to the Arithmetic Theory of Automorphic Functions' ISBN 978-0-691-08092-5 (CM theory for Hilbert modular forms, L-function factorization); Weil, A. (1967) 'Basic Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-90420-7 (Hecke characters, local L-factors); LMFDB Collaboration (2024) doi:10.1007/978-3-031-33460-0 (data source for eigenvalues of 2.2.5.1-125.1-a). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For the CM Hilbert modular form f = 2.2.5.1-125.1-a over F=Q(sqrt(5)), the Hecke character factorization L(s,f) = L(s,psi)*L(s,psibar) implies Ramanujan EQUALITY at every split prime p (i.e., p ≡ 1 mod 5): the Frobenius characteristic polynomial X^2 - a_p*X + N(p) has discriminant Delta = a_p^2 - 4p that is strictly negative at BOTH real embeddings sigma_1, sigma_2 of Q(sqrt(5))/Q. This means both roots are genuinely complex with |alpha|=|alphabar|=sqrt(p) (equality, not merely bound). For inert primes (p ≢ 1 mod 5): a_p = 0 (exact CM zero pattern). The discriminant resonance: eigenvalue e = -3+5*phi (phi=(1+sqrt5)/2) satisfies e^2+e-31=0 and disc(e/Q) = Tr(e)^2 - 4*N(e) = 1 + 124 = 125 = disc(Q(zeta_5)/Q) = level norm of the form. This caps the Langlands ladder: [394] GL1 Frobenius -> [395] GL2 Weil bound -> [396] Z[zeta5] infrastructure -> [397] CM relative norm -> [399] CM form identification -> [403] Frobenius Ramanujan equality. Theorem NT: all arithmetic in Z[phi] (integers), no floats in QA layer; sigma embeddings are observer projections for sign-checking only. Z[phi] representation: phi=(1+sqrt5)/2, element (u,v) = u+v*phi, product law (u1*u2+v1*v2, u1*v2+v1*u2+v1*v2), norm u^2+uv-v^2. Galois conjugation sigma_F: (u,v) -> (u+v,-v). 5 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-12.
